sanskrit:
श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः।
ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति॥
transliteration:
śraddhāvān labhate jñānaṃ tat-paraḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ |
jñānaṃ labdhvā parāṃ śāntim acireṇādhigacchati ||
literal_meaning: A man of faith gains knowledge, intent on That, with senses restrained; having gained knowledge he swiftly attains supreme peace.
contextual_meaning: Cognitive commitment plus somatic self-regulation equals rapid affective stabilization; faith is the expectancy that this process will deliver the existential relief Arjuna seeks.
psychological_state: Hopeful readiness—Arjuna now believes that disciplined attention to Kṛṣṇa’s words will quickly end his inner war.
trigger_scenario: A client finally trusts the therapeutic process, starts daily mindfulness and values-based action, and notices anxiety dropping within weeks.
anchor_text: Trust the process, steady your senses, and knowledge brings swift peace.
